description: Main mathematical definitions that are not part of the base units.

words.name: tau
words.description: Tau (τ) is the circle constant equal to 2π, i.e. the ratio of a circle's circumference to its radius.

words.name: τ
words.description: Symbol for the circle constant tau (2π).

words.name: circle
words.description: A circle is the set of all points in a plane at a given distance (the radius) from a given point (the centre); its circumference equals π times its diameter.

words.name: radius
words.description: The radius of a circle is the distance from its centre to any point on it.

words.name: area
words.description: Area is the quantity that expresses the extent of a two-dimensional region in the plane.

words.name: perimeter
words.description: A perimeter is a closed path that encompasses, surrounds, or outlines a two-dimensional shape.

words.name: volume
words.description: Volume is a measure of three-dimensional space.

words.name: surface
words.description: A surface is a mathematical model of the common concept of a surface, generalising a plane.

words.name: apothem
words.description: The apothem of a regular polygon is a line segment from the centre to the midpoint of one of its sides.

words.name: triangle
words.description: A triangle is a polygon with three corners and three sides.

words.name: rectangle
words.description: A rectangle is a quadrilateral with four right angles.

words.name: square
words.description: A square is a regular quadrilateral with four equal sides and four right angles.

words.name: parallelogram
words.description: A parallelogram is a quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides.

words.name: trapezoid
words.description: A trapezoid is a quadrilateral that has at least one pair of parallel sides.

words.name: ellipse
words.description: An ellipse is a plane curve surrounding two focal points, such that for all points on the curve, the sum of the two distances to the focal points is a constant.

words.name: polygon
words.description: A polygon is a plane figure made up of line segments connected to form a closed polygonal chain.

words.name: cube
words.description: A cube is a three-dimensional solid object bounded by six square faces.

words.name: cylinder
words.description: A cylinder is one of the most basic curvilinear geometric shapes, the surface formed by the points at a fixed distance from a given straight line, the axis of the cylinder.

words.name: sphere
words.description: A sphere is a geometrical object that is a three-dimensional analogue to a two-dimensional circle.

words.name: cone
words.description: A cone is a three-dimensional geometric shape that tapers smoothly from a flat base to a point called the apex or vertex.

words.name: pyramid
words.description: A pyramid is a polyhedron formed by connecting a polygonal base and a point, called the apex.

words.name: prism
words.description: A prism is a polyhedron comprising an n-sided polygon base, a second base which is a translated copy of the first, and n other faces joining corresponding sides of the two bases.

words.name: hypotenuse
words.description: In geometry, the hypotenuse is the longest side of a right triangle, opposite the right angle.

words.name: angle
words.description: In Euclidean geometry, an angle is the figure formed by two rays sharing a common endpoint, called the vertex of the angle.

words.name: sine
words.description: The sine is a trigonometric function defined, for an acute angle in a right-angled triangle, as the ratio of the length of the opposite side to that of the hypotenuse.

words.name: cosine
words.description: The cosine is a trigonometric function defined, for an acute angle in a right-angled triangle, as the ratio of the length of the adjacent side to that of the hypotenuse.

words.name: tangent
words.description: The tangent is a trigonometric function equal to the ratio of sine over cosine.

words.name: radian
words.description: The radian is a unit of plane angle defined such that one full turn equals 2π radians.

words.name: slope
words.description: In mathematics, the slope of a line is a number that describes both the direction and the steepness of the line.

words.name: distance
words.description: Distance is a numerical or occasionally qualitative measurement of how far apart objects, points, people, or ideas are.

words.name: line
words.description: In geometry, a straight line is an infinite length, breadthless, idealised geometric object.

words.name: exponent
words.description: Exponentiation is a mathematical operation involving two numbers: the base and the exponent or power.

words.name: logarithm
words.description: In mathematics, the logarithm is the inverse function to exponentiation.

words.name: e
words.description: The number e, also known as Euler's number, is a mathematical constant approximately equal to 2.71828.

words.name: i
words.description: The imaginary unit i is defined by its property that i² = −1.

words.name: quadratic
words.description: In algebra, a quadratic equation is any equation that can be rearranged in standard form as ax² + bx + c = 0.

words.name: Pythagoras
words.description: Pythagoras of Samos was an ancient Ionian Greek philosopher, polymath and the eponymous founder of Pythagoreanism.

words.name: Euclid
words.description: Euclid was an ancient Greek mathematician active as a geometer and logician.

words.name: Euler
words.description: Leonhard Euler was a Swiss mathematician, physicist, astronomer, geographer, logician and engineer.

words.name: Heron
words.description: Hero of Alexandria was a Greek mathematician and engineer who was active in his native city of Alexandria, Roman Egypt.

words.name: binomial
words.description: In elementary algebra, a binomial is a polynomial that is the sum of two terms.

words.name: sequence
words.description: In mathematics, a sequence is an enumerated collection of objects in which repetitions are allowed and order matters.

words.name: series
words.description: In mathematics, a series is, roughly speaking, an addition of infinitely many terms, one after the other.

words.name: derivative
words.description: The derivative of a function of a real variable measures the sensitivity to change of the function value with respect to a change in its argument.

words.name: integral
words.description: In mathematics, an integral assigns numbers to functions in a way that describes displacement, area, volume, and other concepts that arise by combining infinitesimal data.

words.name: limit
words.description: In mathematics, a limit is the value that a function approaches as the input approaches some value.

words.name: function
words.description: In mathematics, a function from a set X to a set Y assigns to each element of X exactly one element of Y.

words.name: probability
words.description: Probability is the branch of mathematics concerning numerical descriptions of how likely an event is to occur.

words.name: statistics
words.description: Statistics is the discipline that concerns the collection, organization, analysis, interpretation, and presentation of data.

words.name: mean
words.description: There are several kinds of mean in mathematics, especially in statistics; each attempts to summarize or typify a given group of data.

words.name: variance
words.description: In probability theory and statistics, variance is the expectation of the squared deviation of a random variable from its mean.

words.name: deviation
words.description: In mathematics and statistics, deviation is a measure of difference between the observed value of a variable and some other value, often that variable's mean.

words.name: permutation
words.description: In mathematics, a permutation of a set is, loosely speaking, an arrangement of its members into a sequence or linear order.

words.name: combination
words.description: In mathematics, a combination is a selection of items from a set that has distinct members, such that the order of selection does not matter.

words.name: interest
words.description: In finance and economics, interest is payment from a borrower or deposit-taking financial institution to a lender or depositor of an amount above repayment of the principal sum.

words.name: perfect
words.description: describes something complete or exact, e.g. a perfect square

words.name: arc
words.description: a connected portion of the circumference of a circle or other curve

words.name: sector
words.description: the portion of a disk enclosed by two radii and the arc between them

words.name: regular
words.description: describes a polygon with all sides and all angles equal

words.name: identity
words.description: an equality that holds for all values of the variables involved

words.name: double
words.description: twice a given quantity, e.g. a double angle

words.name: law
words.description: a statement that describes a general relationship that always holds

words.name: common
words.description: shared by all members of a sequence, e.g. a common difference or common ratio

words.name: fundamental
words.description: describes a basic, central result, e.g. the fundamental theorem of calculus

words.name: standard
words.description: describes the conventional reference form, e.g. standard deviation

words.name: score
words.description: a standardized value derived from data, e.g. a z score

words.name: conditional
words.description: describes something dependent on a given condition, e.g. conditional probability

words.name: complement
words.description: in set theory and probability, the set of outcomes not in a given event

words.name: union
words.description: in set theory and probability, the set of outcomes in either of two events

words.name: favorable
words.description: the number of outcomes that satisfy a given condition, used in the classical definition of probability

words.name: total
words.description: the whole amount or count, e.g. the total number of possible outcomes

words.name: new
words.description: the current or later value in a comparison, e.g. in percent change

words.name: old
words.description: the previous or earlier value in a comparison, e.g. in percent change

words.name: algebra
words.description: the branch of mathematics dealing with symbols, expressions, equations and their manipulation

words.name: geometry
words.description: the branch of mathematics dealing with shapes, sizes, positions and properties of space

words.name: trigonometry
words.description: the branch of mathematics dealing with the relations between the angles and sides of triangles

words.name: calculus
words.description: the branch of mathematics dealing with limits, derivatives, integrals and continuous change

triples.name: mathematical constant
triples.description: A mathematical constant that never changes e.g. Pi

triples.name: tau (math)
triples.description: Tau is the circle constant equal to 2π.

triples.name: quadratic formula
triples.description: In elementary algebra, the quadratic formula is a closed-form expression describing the solutions of a quadratic equation.

triples.name: Pythagorean theorem
triples.description: In mathematics, the Pythagorean theorem is a fundamental relation in Euclidean geometry between the three sides of a right triangle.

triples.name: Heron's formula
triples.description: In geometry, Heron's formula gives the area of a triangle in terms of the three side lengths.

triples.name: Euler's formula
triples.description: Euler's formula establishes the fundamental relationship between the trigonometric functions and the complex exponential function.

triples.name: binomial theorem
triples.description: In elementary algebra, the binomial theorem describes the algebraic expansion of powers of a binomial.

triples.name: difference of squares
triples.description: In mathematics, the difference of two squares is a squared number subtracted from another squared number.

triples.name: arc length
triples.description: Arc length is the distance between two points along a section of a curve.

triples.name: Pythagorean identity
triples.description: In mathematics, the Pythagorean trigonometric identity expresses the Pythagorean theorem in terms of trigonometric functions.

triples.name: law of sines
triples.description: In trigonometry, the law of sines relates the lengths of the sides of any triangle to the sines of its angles.

triples.name: law of cosines
triples.description: In trigonometry, the law of cosines relates the lengths of the sides of a triangle to the cosine of one of its angles.

triples.name: arithmetic sequence
triples.description: An arithmetic progression is a sequence of numbers such that the difference between the consecutive terms is constant.

triples.name: geometric sequence
triples.description: A geometric progression is a sequence of non-zero numbers where each term after the first is found by multiplying the previous one by a fixed, non-zero number called the common ratio.

triples.name: power rule
triples.description: In calculus, the power rule is used to differentiate functions of the form f(x) = xⁿ.

triples.name: product rule
triples.description: In calculus, the product rule is a formula used to find the derivatives of products of two or more functions.

triples.name: quotient rule
triples.description: In calculus, the quotient rule is a method of finding the derivative of a function that is the ratio of two differentiable functions.

triples.name: chain rule
triples.description: In calculus, the chain rule is a formula that expresses the derivative of the composition of two differentiable functions f and g.

triples.name: fundamental theorem of calculus
triples.description: The fundamental theorem of calculus is a theorem that links the concept of differentiating a function with the concept of integrating a function.

triples.name: integration by parts
triples.description: In calculus, integration by parts is a process that finds the integral of a product of functions in terms of the integral of the product of their derivative and antiderivative.

triples.name: standard deviation
triples.description: In statistics, the standard deviation is a measure of the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of values.

triples.name: z score
triples.description: In statistics, the standard score is the number of standard deviations by which the value of a raw score is above or below the mean value of what is being observed.

triples.name: conditional probability
triples.description: In probability theory, conditional probability is a measure of the probability of an event occurring, given that another event has already occurred.

triples.name: simple interest
triples.description: Simple interest is interest calculated only on the original principal, regardless of how many times the interest is paid.

triples.name: compound interest
triples.description: Compound interest is the interest on a loan or deposit calculated based on both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods.

triples.name: percent change
triples.description: Percentage change is a way of expressing a change in a variable; it represents the relative change between the old value and the new one.

triples.name: financial mathematics
triples.description: the branch of mathematics dealing with interest, value over time and financial calculations. see Wikipedia: Mathematical finance (Q335632)

triples.name: golden ratio
triples.description: the ratio (about 1.618) in which the whole is to the larger part as the larger part is to the smaller

formulas.name: definition of fundamental theorem of calculus
definition of union
formulas.latex: P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B)
